location: IMPORTANT DISCREPANCY FLAG: cross-checking wiki.gg and Fextralife shows this is actually a Shadow of the Erdtree DLC weapon, not a base-game item — it does not exist anywhere in the base game and cannot be obtained without owning the DLC. In the DLC, it's found on a corpse behind an illusory wall inside Lamenter's Gaol, Gravesite Plain: obtain the Gaol Upper Level Key first, use it to get the Gaol Lower Level Key, then pass through the illusory wall to unlock the final door to the corpse.
region: Gravesite Plain — Shadow of the Erdtree DLC region (this location does NOT exist on the base-game map)

source.name: Corpse behind an illusory wall, Lamenter's Gaol (DLC)

tactics: This weapon requires the Shadow of the Erdtree DLC — flag for review, since the source list places it in base/torches.json
Get the Gaol Upper Level Key first (elsewhere in Lamenter's Gaol), trade/use it to obtain the Gaol Lower Level Key
Look for an illusory wall guarding the final locked door — illusory walls in Elden Ring are revealed by attacking or rolling into the wall
Its unique Blindfold of Happiness skill grants roughly 15 seconds of invisibility, useful for slipping past dangerous DLC enemy clusters nearby
lore: A stone lantern shaped like a grieving human face, its hollow eyes glowing softly. Despite the tormented expression carved into the stone, the light it casts feels oddly calm — like someone who has stopped needing to see.

builds.name: Pure Strength build
builds.why: Unusually high Strength scaling for a torch makes it a legitimate physical-damage option for STR-heavy characters rather than just a light source.

builds.name: Stealth exploration utility
builds.why: The Blindfold of Happiness skill's temporary invisibility suits any build that wants to bypass a dangerous room rather than fight through it.
